issue:
HOST PRDW3456
OFFLINE
HOST PRDW3459
OFFLINE
****
ALL 152 PROD HOSTS OFFLINE

answer.title: RESPOND
answer.detail: You don't need an SLO to tell you that everything in prod being down is bad. Better check it out.

issue: 90% DISK UTILIZATION ON DB NODE 3

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: Unless customers complain, it's impossible to know if this is causing a real issue. Remember to add an SLO to avoid this recurring noisy alert.

issue: DEPLOY PIPELINE IS BROKEN

answer.title: IT DEPENDS
answer.detail: The dependency various organizations have on their deployment pipeline can vary wildly, so either option is correct.

issue: NO DATA: EPHEMERAL STORAGE IS ABNORMALLY HIGH ON HOST: 1-0270DEE16E37AC586

answer.title: RESPOND
answer.detail: Sometimes it's hard to tell if your workloads and clusters are impacted by issues far down in the stack. Better to respond and investigate — ideally setting up monitoring and an SLO to avoid this recurring noisy alert.

issue: YOUR ERROR BUDGET WOULD BE EXHAUSTED IN 2 HOURS AND THE CONDITION LASTS FOR 10 MINUTES

answer.title: RESPOND
answer.detail: SLO alerts should be actionable. Your error budget is burning fast and you are on pace to run out of it in the next 2 hours at this rate. You should probably get up and take care of this.

issue:
SERVICE RETURNING 502s
ERROR BUDGET REMAINING: -165%
BURN RATE: 5x

answer.title: RESPOND
answer.detail: The burn rate indicates you're having a real issue. You should investigate now.

issue: CEO SAYS THE SITE ISN'T LOADING FOR THEM

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: Your CEO is but one of many users (albeit an important one). It's more likely it's an issue with their wifi or setup than real downtime (unless you have other signals).

issue: HTTP 500 ERRORS LEADING TO BUDGET BURN OF 10x NORMAL

answer.title: RESPOND
answer.detail: Your service is either completely down or performing terribly and likely requires immediate human intervention.

issue:
EURAPP STATUS:
1 HOST = DOWN
9 HOST = UP

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: SLOs provide a buffer for a certain amount of expected failure. You can use them to let you know if one host out of ten is actually causing your users problems or if you can absorb this failure and check on things later.

issue: SERVICE RETURNING 502s, ERROR BUDGET REMAINING: -20%, BURN RATE 0x

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: 0x Burn rate is the giveaway. While we've been out of error budget for a while, this SLO is actually improving as bad data points are dropping out of the tracking window.

issue: ACME CLOUD PROVIDER STATUS: INCIDENT: WE ARE INVESTIGATING REPORTS OF DEGRADED AVAILABILITY

answer.title: IT DEPENDS
answer.detail: If you haven't gotten other alerts, then everything failed over properly, and you can go back to sleep. But will you really be able to?

issue: RESPONSE LATENCY INCREASED ON AN API SERVICE. ERROR BUDGET IS BURNING. ERROR BUDGET REMAINING: 14%. BURN RATE 0.91x

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: A burn rate under 1x means you're not actually going to exceed your error budget. You can go back to bed and tune this alert later.

issue: SYNTHETIC LATENCY SLO SHOWS SLOW BURN. ERROR BUDGET REMAINING: 8%. BURN RATE: 1.5%

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: If you're burning slowly, you can probably wait until business hours to investigate.

issue: SYNTHETIC LATENCY SLO SHOWS FAST BURN. ERROR BUDGET REMAINING: 24%. BURN RATE: 5x

answer.title: RESPOND
answer.detail: If you're burning error budget quickly, you probably need to take a look at things.

issue: P0 TICKET OPENED BY CUSTOMER SUPPORT: MANY CUSTOMERS COMPLAINING ON TWITTER

answer.title: RESPOND
answer.detail: When your customers are actively complaining about you online, you definitely need to take a look.

issue: WEB UI PAGES FAILING TO LOAD. ERROR BUDGET REMINAING: 3%. BURN RATE: 1x

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: Errors are always going to happen. That is why we have error budgets. A burn that isn't fast burn is not necessarily bad. Having 1x burn on a calendar-aligned time window means you are maximizing your error budget.

issue: SSL CERT EXPIREDS IN 7 DAYS

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: Wait until the morning. Ideally, an error budget alert could file a ticket or bug, so you don't forget.

issue:
HOST STGW3456 OFFLINE
HOST STGW3459 OFFLINE
ALL 157 STAGE HOSTS OFFLINE

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: Wait until the morning... unless you treat staging as production.

issue: 99% CPU UTILIZATION ON LNXSRV1 OVER THE LAST 10 MINUTES

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: If you have meaningful SLOs, you'll find out when this actually causes problems for users. You can investigate in the morning.

issue: API RESPONSES HTTP ERROR RATE: 7.5%

answer.title: IT DEPENDS
answer.detail: 7.5% is actually reasonable. Or maybe it's terrible! It can be difficult to tell in the middle of the night if you don't have SLO targets to help you figure it out.

issue: KUBERNETES ERROR: PRODENVPOD-4986 IS OOMKILLED

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: A single pod being oomkilled means you should probably investigate things. But not at 03:00 in the morning! When you have SLOs, you can have error budget burn rates alert you when OOM problems are causing your customers problems.

issue: PRDXSADB CLUSTER HAS FAILED OVER.

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: This can be addressed in the AM, tha's the point of a cluster right? SLOs will account for the temporarily poor performance. Set an alert if you're burning error budget too fast.

issue: P95 LATENCY EXCEEDS 400ms

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: Even though latency temporarily went over 400ms, it quickly returned to normal. Using an SLO would reduce false alarms like this alert. Safely ignore.

issue: MAINTENANCE: COMPLETED PLATFORM MANTENANCE IN FR (BLUE) REGION. THE SCHEDULE MAINTENANCE HAS BEEN COMPLETED. NO ISSUES FOUND

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: Why does this page even exist?!? Get a good night's rest, my friend.

issue: BID PRICE OF SPOT REQUEST LOWER THAN CURRENT SPOT PRICE. ERROR BUDGET REMAINING: 85%. BURN RATE 0x

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: Eror budget looks fine. Whatever wants to run on those spot instances will have to wait.

issue: END USER APPDEX BELOW 0.8

answer.title: DEPENDS
answer.detail: It looks like this has recovered, right? Is there something that tracks whether this has been misbehaving enough to erode user confidence? Like, say, an SLO?

issue: URG: NETWORK BANDWIDGET LIMIT EXCEEDED. THROTTLING.

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: If your traffic is getting throttled, that means that your users might also be experiencing increased latency. But a good error budget alert would let you know when it's actually causing them too many problems.

issue: YOU BREACHED YOUR ERROR BUDGET A SECOND TIME AFTER RESOLVING THE FIRST BREACH. ERROR BUDGET REMAINING: -8%. BURN RATE 0x

answer.title: RESPOND
answer.detail: Welp, you have clearly ciolated your SO again. Had you set your alert policy, you could have saved the error budget before this happened. Are you sure you didn't sleep through an earlier page?

issue: URG: TRAFFIC THRESHOLD EXCEEDED 10000 QPS

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: If the increased traffic isn't affecting the user experience and your SLOs are stready, you can safely go back to bed. Who cares if traffic threshold alerts went off if nothing is impacted?!

issue: DNS SYNTHETIC TEST: PRODUCTION APP = FAILED

answer.title: RESPOND
answer.detail: It could just be an issue with your check, but if there's an issue with the DNS records, users could not be able to access the site. Probably best to get up and check it out.

issue: APPNOD3: CRASHLOOPBACKOFF

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: If it doesn't affect any of your SLOs or the user experience, you can wait until the morning to investigate.

issue: YOUR IRS REFUND IS PENDING ACCEPTANCE! MUST ACCEPT BY GOING TO HTTPS://BIT.LY/3Z8RN95 IN 24 HOURS.

answer.title: IGNORE
answer.detail: OOPS! Sometimes you get pages by spam texts or emails!
